steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The scientist calls BZMA the best option when "practical considerations" (cost) are weighed in. The correct choice must show BZMA nearly matching the expensive β-cyclodextrin resin while clearly outperforming the other cheap methacrylate resins — proving best value.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "For both toluene and trichloromethane, the BZMA-derived resin exhibited only modestly lower absorption capacity than the resin synthesized from β-cyclodextrin but higher absorption capacity than other resins consisting of commercially available methacrylate monomers.": Shows the cost-benefit trade-off directly. ✓
  • "Relative to the resin synthesized from β-cyclodextrin and to other resins consisting of commercially available methacrylate monomers, the BZMA-derived resin exhibited superior absorption capacity for toluene but not for trichloromethane.": Performance varies by compound, undermining the general claim. ✗
  • "For both toluene and trichloromethane, the BZMA-derived resin exhibited similar absorption capacity as other resins consisting of commercially available methacrylate monomers and a slightly higher absorption capacity than the resin synthesized from β-cyclodextrin.": This does not show why BZMA specifically has the greatest potential; it performs only similarly to the other cheaper methacrylate resins, so it fails to distinguish BZMA from its low-cost peers. ✗
  • "Whereas the resin synthesized from β-cyclodextrin exhibited the highest absorption capacity for toluene, the BZMA-derived resin and other resins consisting of commercially available methacrylate monomers exhibited the highest absorption capacity for trichloromethane.": BZMA doesn't stand out from other inexpensive options, so it has no special value. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
